title: THE NOTION OF DIPLOMATIC PROTOCOL AND ITS ROLE IN MODERN INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS
author: Rustamova Sevinch Mirzoxid qizi , Abdurahmonov Turahon Saydullayevich
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Diplomatic protocol is a set of formal rules and conventions that govern interactions between states and their representatives. It ensures that diplomatic relations are conducted with respect, order, and predictability. In modern international relations, adherence to diplomatic protocol is crucial for maintaining effective communication, preventing misunderstandings, and fostering cooperation between countries. Violations or neglect of these protocols can lead to diplomatic tensions and misunderstandings, potentially affecting international stability. This paper examines the concept of diplomatic protocol, its historical development, and its significance in contemporary diplomacy, highlighting the challenges faced by diplomats in adhering to these formalities while managing complex international relations.
